item.title: from a Trump supporter: ‘Why do liberals think Trump supporters are stupid?’
item.description: [EDIT TO FIX LINKS] An anguished question from a Trump supporter: ‘Why do liberals think Trump supporters are stupid?’ THE SERIOUS ANSWER: Here’s what the majority of anti-Trump voters honestly feel about Trump supporters en masse: That when you saw a man who had owned a fraudulent University, intent on scamming poor people, you thought "Fine." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/onpolitics/2018/04/10/trump-university-settlement-judge-finalized/502387002/) That when you saw a man who had made it his business practice to stiff his creditors, you said, "Okay." ( https://www.thedailybeast.com/trump-hotel-paid-millions-in-fines-for-unpaid-work?fbclid=IwAR3HrrHWioVilc1-gUoxLZNVyyrl1eeZJ28tEkZ8CG0UrTk5BVBFF8yKDuQ) That when you heard him proudly brag about his own history of sexual abuse, you said, "No problem." (https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/list-trumps-accusers-allegations-sexual-misconduct/story?id=51956410) That when he made up stories about seeing Muslim-Americans in the thousands cheering the destruction of the World Trade Center, you said, "Not an issue." (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/fact-checker/wp/2015/11/22/donald-trumps-outrageous-claim-that-thousands-of-new-jersey-muslims-celebrated-the-911-attacks/) That when you saw him brag that he could shoot a man on Fifth Avenue and you wouldn't care, you exclaimed, "He sure knows me." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2019/10/23/president-donald-trump-could-shoot-someone-without-prosecution/4073405002/) That when you heard him relating a story of an elderly guest of his country club, an 80-year old man, who fell off a stage and hit his head, to Trump replied: “‘Oh my God, that’s disgusting,’ and I turned away. I couldn’t—you know, he was right in front of me, and I turned away. I didn’t want to touch him. He was bleeding all over the place. And I felt terrible, because it was a beautiful white marble floor, and now it had changed color. Became very red.” You said, "That's cool!" (https://www.gq.com/story/donald-trump-howard-stern-story) That when you saw him mock the disabled, you thought it was the funniest thing you ever saw. (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2016-election/new-york-times-slams-donald-trump-after-he-appears-mock-n470016) That when you heard him brag that he doesn't read books, you said, "Well, who has time?" (https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2018/01/americas-first-post-text-president/549794/) That when the Central Park Five were compensated as innocent men convicted of a crime they didn't commit, and he angrily said that they should still be in prison, you said, "That makes sense." (https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2019/06/19/what-trump-has-said-central-park-five/1501321001/) That when you heard him tell his supporters to beat up protesters and that he would hire attorneys, you thought, "Yes!" (https://www.latimes.com/politics/la-na-trump-campaign-protests-20160313-story.html) That when you heard him tell one rally to confiscate a man's coat before throwing him out into the freezing cold, you said, "What a great guy!" (https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/donald-trump-orders-protester-s-coat-is-confiscated-and-he-is-sent-into-the-cold-a6802756.html) That you have watched the parade of neo-Nazis and white supremacists with whom he curries favor, while refusing to condemn outright Nazis, and you have said, "Thumbs up!" (https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2018/08/why-cant-trump-just-condemn-nazis/567320/) That you hear him unable to talk to foreign dignitaries without insulting their countries and demanding that they praise his electoral win, you said, "That's the way I want my President to be." (https://www.huffpost.com/entry/trump-insult-foreign-countries-leaders_n_59dd2769e4b0b26332e76d57) That you have watched him remove expertise from all layers of government in favor of people who make money off of eliminating protections in the industries they're supposed to be regulating and you have said, "What a genius!" (https://www.politico.com/agenda/story/2017/12/29/138-trump-policy-changes-2017-000603) That you have heard him continue to profit from his businesses, in part by leveraging his position as President, to the point of overcharging the Secret Service for space in the properties he owns, and you have said, "That's smart!" (https://www.usnews.com/opinion/articles/2018-03-05/how-is-donald-trump-profiting-from-the-presidency-let-us-count-the-ways) That you have heard him say that it was difficult to help Puerto Rico because it was in the middle of water and you have said, "That makes sense." (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/politics/wp/2017/09/26/the-very-big-ocean-between-here-and-puerto-rico-is-not-a-perfect-excuse-for-a-lack-of-aid/) That you have seen him start fights with every country from Canada to New Zealand while praising Russia and quote, "falling in love" with the dictator of North Korea, and you have said, "That's statesmanship!" (https://www.cnn.com/2019/07/02/politics/donald-trump-dictators-kim-jong-un-vladimir-putin/index.html) That Trump separated children from their families and put them in cages, managed to lose track of 1500 kids, has opened a tent city incarceration camp in the desert in Texas - he explains that they’re just “animals” - and you say, “Well, OK then.” ( https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/more-5-400-children-split-border-according-new-count-n1071791 ) That you have witnessed all the thousand and one other manifestations of corruption and low moral character and outright animalistic rudeness and contempt for you, the working American voter, and you still show up grinning and wearing your MAGA hats and threatening to beat up anybody who says otherwise. (https://www.americanprogress.org/article/confronting-cost-trumps-corruption-american-families/) What you don't get, Trump supporters, is that our succumbing to frustration and shaking our heads, thinking of you as stupid, may very well be wrong and unhelpful, but it's also...hear me...charitable. Because if you're NOT stupid, we must turn to other explanations, and most of them are less flattering. - Adam-Troy Castro
item.dc:creator: WalkGood

item.title: Many others will do the same
item.description: I just finished my 22nd year of teaching. I'm the sole income in my home. If I had any other option, I'd likely be out the door, too. This is an unsustainable system.
item.dc:creator: mustbethedragon

item.title: Unconditional Love
item.description: This happened a few months ago, and she is actually now my fiancee. Happy Pride, y'all! I'm editing to add this: please don't comment "You could leave them if you wanted to". I know that. I am not with her because I feel required to be. I wanted to marry this person and them transitioning didn't change that . Our relationship is airtight and incredibly wholesome and healthy. 2nd edit: Thanks for the accolade!! 3: ANOTHER accolade‽ You guuuuuuuuys!
item.dc:creator: FrecklesIRL

item.title: Too Accurate!
item.description: This happened to me when hubby and I went to Home Depot. The guy told me "Don't worry little lady, he won't make a big mess." While I was the one who was going to fix the plumbing issue! ? https://twitter.com/kristabellerina/status/1532047303409750017?t=yfvd3EqXzNgdL4DUtml2Gg&s=19

item.dc:creator: partyspooper

item.title: My painting displayed at a hobby shop!
item.description:
Pretty cool milestone for me as an artist. I now have my work displayed in our local hobby shop that holds huge Warhammer 40k tournaments. I’ve not put my work out there too much in public settings, but this is pretty cool to see when I walk in the shop. The scene was painted with the approval of the Skyrim concept artist, Darek Zabrocki. He is an absolutely incredible digital artist.
Most viral edit!? I post a lot of my stuff here, but if you do have Instagram, feel free to follow along a little closer at www.instagram.com/paintings_by_adam_stevens As always, I appreciate the Imgur community’s support on all of my artwork the past few years. Thanks so much.
item.dc:creator: PilotBuilder

item.title: In honor of Pride: Celebrities supporting their trans family members
item.description:
The Mandalorian himself, Pedro Pascal, with his sister Lux. In regards to supporting family members, all I can say is: “This is the way”
The Scream Queen herself, Jamie Lee Curtis, with her daughter Ruby. Jamie’s been a lovely and supportive mom and even officiated her daughter’s wedding!
Here’s Charlize Theron with her daughters, on the left is Jackson, who came out when she was just 3 years old. Witness!
Last we have Colin Mochrie, with his daughter Kylie. Colin’s been very outspoken in his support of Kylie. Remember: Support from your family and friends means a lot. ?️‍⚧️
item.dc:creator: GabbiTheSwedishNerdGirl

item.title: There are other ways to win a game
item.dc:creator: minPD0309

item.title: No one cares. But I feel it needs to be heard.
item.description: “We’d do anything to protect our kids” “okay pick policies that might raise your taxes” “NEVER” it’s really starting to get old and I’m so tired and literally nothing changes. I’m scared for my kid, and none of this is helping that. The most frustrating part: husband is pro gun. When I asked him if he still was he basically said he’s scared to give them up because republicans/cops could still come for us and in situations like Ukraine they couldn’t distribute guns fast enough. Imagine being so scared in your own country that you won’t even consider giving up weapons to stop children being hunted for sport. Idk. I just feel like I’m sitting here screaming and no one cares. This is like the fifth gigantic traumatizing event I’ve suffered through because Texas and/or the US sucks (I grew up going to Uvalde all the time so this hits really hard, and also worked in disaster relief, huge yikes) and I am sick of being called selfish by my family when I talk about moving to a country where CHILDREN DONT DIE.
item.dc:creator: kirbyvictorious

item.title: JFC America
item.description: Every time I think we've reached a new low I see shit like this and just get that sinking feeling. And yes, this is real: https://www.thedailybeast.com/the-supreme-court-just-said-in-in-shinn-v-ramirez-that-evidence-of-innocence-is-not-enough The vote was 6-3. Edit: A couple people have mentioned this so I do want to give a content warning for the article above. It contains an extremely graphic and upsetting description of the crime that the defendant is being accused of. Although I really think there is insufficient evidence that the defendant is the one who committed this crime, it was still a deeply fucked up thing. Victims deserve justice, especially in cases like this. One of the worst aspects of this ruling is that it will ensure that innocent people will be put in jail with no real evidence, while actual perpetrators walk free.
item.dc:creator: Freyja33

item.title: Anyone else?
item.description: I've worked at icecream shop for 11 years and one of our products is a "superbowl", pre trump era we always use to put an American flag in it, but ever since the clown I feel awkward using my own countries flag. Feels like I'm siding with them when I use it. Just feel sad about how my pride for my own country was hijacked by a domestic terrorist group.

item.dc:creator: BootsDusty

item.title: Pride: The burnt books
item.description:
It's Pride of the twenty-second year of the twenty-first century, and so I feel it is important to draw attention to which books famously burnt in Berlin in the thirty-third year of the twentieth century. Dr Hirschfeld was a pioneer in a huge range of areas relating to human sexuality and founded the world's first LGBT-rights organisation - the Scientific-Humanitarian Committee in 1897 (one hundred and twenty five years ago). He later founded the Berlin Institut für Sexualwissenschaft in 1919 & the World League for Sexual Reform in 1921. As well as engaging in academic study, his organisations and institute provided aid of all kinds to sexual minorities in the new liberal atmosphere of Weimar Germany, clinical staff included psychiatrists Felix Abraham (1901-1937) and Arthur Kronfeld (1886-1941), gynecologist Ludwig Levy-Lenz (1892-1966), dermatologist and endocrinologist Bernhard Schapiro (1888-1966) and dermatologist Friedrich Wertheim (?-?), almost all of whom were engaged in pioneering (if sometimes fruitless) study into human sexuality. This made Berlin the capital of LGBT+ culture, science, and empathy.
[Picture: A 1902 collection of articles on homosexuality and transgenderism from the library of the Scientific-Humanitarian Committee/Institut für Sexualwissenschaft. The notes, stamps and stickers tell a tale of attempted obliteration and erasure. This copy was confiscated on 6 May 1933, the pencil note on the left side says: "By Reichspräsident's decree of 28.02.1933 (The "Reichstag fire decree") destined for destruction!"; it survived this order and ended up in the Prussian State Library's collection, where it was hidden from the public with the label "Secr(et)". It is perhaps telling that I can find no information whatsoever about who author "August Fleischmann" (likely a pseudonym) was.] His institute also offered some of the world's first sex reassignment surgeries and employed transgender people, who lived and worked in the institute as their chosen gender*. Understanding of the hormonal influence on development was still in its infancy, but Hirschfeld grasped quickly the idea that if a person's "glandular orchestra" was discordant with their mental identity, it might be able to artificially adjust medically, leading to tentative hormonal therapies (this approach was also embraced by the Nazis to opposite ends, in 1944 Danish doctor Carl Værnet (1893-1965) inserted artificial testosterone glands into nonconsenting male concentration camp inmates to try and "cure" their homosexuality). Hirschfeld successfully negotiated a deal between the Institute and the Berlin Police that allowed those registered as 'patients' of the Institute to live publicly as their chosen gender, without risk of being arrested for public indecency. He also supported the legalisation of abortion, and was active in the early German feminist movement led by Helene Stöcker (1869-1943). By the mid 1920s, Hirschfeld was nicknamed "the Einstein of Sex", with a careful media strategy of focusing on demystifying heterosexual sexual issues (such as sexual fulfilment in marriage) while continually advocating acceptance of other sexualities. Hirschfeld was motivated both by his own homosexuality** and by his work as a psychiatrist with other homosexuals & sexual minorities, who were significantly more likely to display evidence of self-harm and suicide attempts, which fired him with righteous indignation at the lack of understanding.
[Picture: Students of the German University of Physical Education, organised by the Nazi party, parade in front of the Institut für Sexualwissenschaft prior to its looting and destruction. The Institute's entrance is "guarded" by members of the SA brownshirts - the paramilitary wing of the Nazi party which was somewhat ironically led by the homosexual Ernst Rohm; this form of intimidation was also enacted against Jewish-owned businesses, as the brownshirts would take down the identities of people trying to enter.] Hirschfeld's work flourished until the rise of the Nazi party in the mid-1930s; as a Jew, he became a Nazi bogeyman - an avatar of decadent and erosive "Jewish intellectualism", leading to his Institute being attacked by the Nazis on 6 May 1933. The vast archive, taking in both the library of the Institute and that of the Scientific-Humanitarian Committee, was stolen - the greatest collection of early 20th century understanding of sexual minorities in the world, a Library of Alexandria consisting of 20,000 volumes (many unique, including bound collections of now-lost pamphlets & political campaigning materials), 35,000 glass photographic slides, and thousands of personal clinical files - to be burnt in front of 40,000 Germans while Joseph Goebbels ranted over the flames on 10 May 1933. This destruction effectively erased the world's first organised attempt to recognise, record, and research LGBT+ human life and likely "turned back the clock" on LGBT+ acceptance in the Euro-American world by, at minimum, thirty years. The invisibility of LGBT+ people in the historical record, often used to dismiss claims of LGBT+ humanity even today, is one direct result of this Nazi attack.
[Picture: footage of the 10 May 1933 book-burnings, a nationwide event against "Un-German" literature, with bonfires across the country, a great majority of that seen here being destroyed in Berlin was from the Hirschfeld collection. Goebbels, seen at the end of the clip, announced this heralded the end of an era of "Jewish intellectualism".] It is also likely that Dora Richter (b.1891), who underwent (in surgeries over a decade) the world's first first male-to-female sex reassignment, was killed in this attack, as she worked live-in as a maid, and disappears from history on 6 May 1933 alongside the books. The painstakingly-negotiated records kept by the Berlin Police of transgender "patients" of the Institute were eventually turned over to the new Reich Central Office for the Combating of Homosexuality and Abortion (founded 10 October 1936), and used to aid in the identifying and capture of these "asocial deviants" in the early days of the concentration camp system. Dr Hirschfeld was in Paris at the time of the attack, and did not return to Germany. He commented that watching newsreel footage of the burnings felt like witnessing his own funeral. He lived the final years of his life in exile in a polyamorous relationship with two other men. Dr Hirschfeld's boyfriend Karl Giese lived with both Hirschfeld and his other boyfriend (Tao Li - see below) in Paris until 1934, at which point he was involved in a 'trifling' bathhouse offence, for which the French authorities revoked his residence, meaning he was forced to leave to go to Austria, where he was later forced to flee after Austria unified with Nazi Germany. He committed suicide in 1938 in response to the Nazi invasion of Czechoslovakia, where he had fled to.
[Picture: Nazis salute above the smouldering remnants of 'Un-German' literature, including now forever-unknown studies and stories of LGBT+ life from the late Victorian era to the 1930s, Berlin 10 May 1933.] The other of Dr Hirschfeld's long-term boyfriends, Hong Konger Tao Li / Li Shiu Tong*** is something of a mysterious figure, disappearing from history after Hirschfeld's death until his own death in Vancouver, Canada in 1993, at which point his personal effects - photographs, some of Hirschfeld's diaries, books from the Institute's collection and Dr Hirschfeld's death-mask(!) - were collected from a dumpster by a curious member of the public, who later shared images of them online, to the amazement of historians who rushed to reclaim the items and return them to Berlin.
[Picture: Grave of Henry Gerber, the man who set up the US' first LGBT+ rights organisation, at the United States Soldiers' and Airmen's Home National Cemetery in Washington DC.] Henry Gerber (1892-1972), an American soldier operating as part of the post-WW1 American army of occupation in Germany, met Dr Hirschfield and was so impressed and inspired by him that when Gerber returned to Chicago, he started the United States' first known gay rights organisation, the Society for Human Rights, in 1924. An effort is ongoing to recover as much as possible of Hirschfeld's scattered records and studies, and gather them together at the Magnus Hirschfeld Society in Berlin, defying the Nazi attempt to eliminate from history his work, and the lives of those he studied, aided, and increased empathy of. In 2011, the Federal Government of Germany was pressured into donating millions of Euros to aid this work, having previously outrageously decided that the Nazi destruction of Hirschfeld's work was legal, and so not capable of being compensated for.
[Video: Dr Hirschfeld (right) appearing as himself in "Different from the Others" - a movie now one hundred and three years old.] Dr Hirschfeld featured as "the sexologist" in the 1919 film "Different from the Others", a film partially funded by his institute, the first pro-gay film in world history, which appealed against the Prussia legal code 175 that outlawed homosexuality, and in particular drew explicit attention to the link between lack of acceptance of different sexualities and suicide.
[Picture: "Through knowledge, to justice".] At the end of the film, after the protagonist Paul Körner commits suicide, his lover Kurt is also planning on killing himself, when Hirschfeld appears to tell him: "If you want to honour the memory of your dead friend, you must not take your own life, but instead preserve it to change the prejudices whose victim – one of the countless many – this dead man was. That is the task of the living I assign you. ... what matters now is to restore honour and justice to the many thousands before us, with us and after us. Through knowledge to justice!" The struggle goes on. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Notes: *this still took place within the understanding of gender norms at the time, so Dora Richter, before her pioneering sex-reassignment surgery in 1922-31, worked at the institute as a maid. ** Hirschfeld gained a classic gay-slang 'femme name' - "Aunt Magnesia" *** Tao Li means "beloved disciple", a pet-name. ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- What happened next: "Rainbows are bulletproof" https://imgur.com/gallery/xIbrOJW Putting the pieces back together: "Drunk Googling" https://imgur.com/gallery/Goiunle
item.dc:creator: BodicaRexVII

item.title: Since February 2022 , I have…
item.description: It’s the second job interview out of 98 applications sent. Wish me luck y’all! FP Edit: I feel that the interview turned out well. What was expected to be a 30 minute interview lasted 53 minutes. The HR person said it was “a fun interview”. Is there anyone in HR out there who can tell me if I did well or not from this? I’m expecting to hear back from them in 10 business days. Edit too: I meant to say “I have an interview tomorrow”.
item.dc:creator: BanditHeelerSayingOhBiscuits

item.title: Happy birthday Morena Baccarin
item.dc:creator: themadchopper
